content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662            322-5660            322-5901            322-6441            272-6444

November 18, 1980


Salvatore Leonardi
3955 Valerie Drive
Campbell, CA 95008

A-80-11-111

Dear Mr. Leonardi:

This is in response to your letter dated August 4,
1980, and our recent telephone conversation of October 30,
1980, regarding the disqualification provision of the Political
Reform Act. The facts as I understand them are as follows.

You are presently the Fire Marshal of the City of Campbell.
Part of your responsibilities include inspecting the building
plans of developers and other builders to determine if their
proposed facilities comply with the fire code. It is your
intention to start a fire protection consulting firm that
will provide services to developers, architects and other
building designers. The type of services provided by your
firm will include reviewing proposed building plans of clients
and determining what actions must be taken by them in order
that they might comply with existing fire codes. Your firm
will also provide instruction in the use of fire extinguishers
and other fire protection devices as well as provide training
in first aid. It is your intention to perform the above
described services on projects of businesses that are outside
the City of Campbell. However, some projects may involve
businesses that maintain offices in Campbell. You have
asked our office to determine if there is any possible conflict
of interest if you form this company.

Government Code Section 87100 provides:

No public official at any level of state or local
government shall make, participate in making or in any
way attempt to use his official position to influence a
governmental decision in which he knows or has reason
to know he has a financial interest.

87100

Salvatore Leonardi
November 18, 1980
Page Two


Government Code Section 87103 provides in part that an
official has a financial interest in a governmental decision
if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will mate-
rially affect a business entity in which he or she has an
investment of $1,000 or more or the decision will materially
affect a source of income from which the official, or a
business entity in which he or she has a 10 percent interest
or greater, has received $250 or more within 12 months of
the decision pending before the official.

It is not a conflict of interest under the Political
Reform Act for you to establish your consulting firm. The
Act in this instance will require you as Fire Marshal to
refrain from participating in those governmental decisions
that will materially affect the consulting firm. Assuming
that you will own 10 percent or more of the firm, you will
also have to refrain from participating in any decision that
affects any of the firm's clients that have paid it $250 or
more during the previous 12 months.

I hope that this letter is helpful. If I can be of
further assistance regarding this matter, please do not
hesitate to contact me.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Dwight E. Dickerson
Counsel,
Legal Division

DED:plh

3955 Valerie Drive
Campbell, CA 95008
August 4, 1980


Dwight Dickerson, Attorney at Law
Conflict of Interest Section
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Mr. Dickerson:

City Clerk Phyllis Acker has advised me of the telephone conversation
that she had with you regarding my intention to start a fire protection
consulting firm. I would appreciate your reviewing the following
information and advising me as to any possible conflict of interest in
forming the company.

It is my intention to start a company that will provide a fire protection
consulting service to developers, architects, building designers, etc.
This work will be done for proposed developments outside the City Limits
of Campbell. I may, however, from time to time, be dealing with companies
that have their permanent offices, mailing address, etc. in the City of
Campbell.

The type of service provided will include such things as the following:

1. Review conceptual drawings and make recommendations and/or advise
when there are some fire protection needs for such a development.

2. From time to time, visit the Fire Marshal of the City involved and
ascertain any out-of-the ordinary requirements that may be over and
above the standard code requirements.

3. Submit a report to the architect, builders, etc. of my findings and
explain acceptable alternatives that would be allowed under the
regulations of that city.

4. Provide instruction to employees of businesses in the handling of
portable fire extinguishers and other fire protection devices, such
as hose lines, breathing masks, etc.

5. Provide instruction to employees of businesses in the administration
of first aid, mouth-to-mouth resuscitation and cardio-pulmonary
resuscitation.

Dwight Dickerson, Attorney at Law
Fair Political Practices Commission
Page Two


6. Provide business managers with a fire protection update service, wherein they would be reminded when certain devices and equipment were due for the annual maintenance check, and arrange with other licensed companies to come and do the maintenance work. No actual repair work would be done by anyone in my employ.

These are just a few of the functions that I would propose. I do realize that I must use discretion on what I would consider a direct conflict with my role as the City Fire Marshal.

Very truly yours,

[Signature]
Salvatore Leonardi
Battalion Chief

Position

Assistant to the City Manager
Administrative Assistant II
Administrative Assistant I
City Attorney
Community Services Coordinator
City Clerk
City Treasurer
Police Chief
Police Captain
Police Lieutenant
Building Official
Senior Building Inspector
Building Inspector
Finance Director
Accounting Supervisor
Purchasing Assistant
Fire Chief
Battalion Chief
Public Works Director
Engineering Manager
Superintendent of Public Works
Associate Civil Engineer
Senior Engineering Technician
Assistant Civil Engineer
Public Works Inspector
Planning Director
Senior Planner
Planner II
Planner I
H.C.D. Grant Coordinator
Board of Housing & Building Code Appeals
Planning Commission
Historic Preservation Board
Site and Architectural Review Committee

Exhibit
4

EXHIBIT B

DISCLOSURE CATEGORIES

This exhibit represents a general summary only. Detailed instructions
and reporting requirements for the categories are to be found in the preceding
Section 5 of this Code, and in the "Instruction Manual for Statement of
Economic Interests."

1. Persons in this category shall disclose all interests in real
property located within the City of Campbell, or within two miles of the
city boundary (not to include the principal residence of the person).

2. Persons in this category shall disclose all sources of income and
investments in business entities located within the City of Campbell.

3. Persons in this category shall disclose all investments in business
entities or income from sources in the construction or building industry
doing business within the City of Campbell.

4. Persons in this category shall disclose all investments in business
entities or income from sources which manufacture or sell supplies of the
type utilized by the department for which the designated employee is employed.

Exhibit B
